spec.title: IGDB and Wikidata platform language adapters

humanSummary.text: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers after adding the platform-language demotion read model. One P2 source-safety follow-up remains for repository-derived conflict augmentation.

findings.title: Official-English conflicts are fixture-authored rather than repository-derived
findings.description: Recorded IGDB/Wikidata adapters import conflict metadata from fixture payloads; broader repository-derived language conflict augmentation remains future work.

evidence.detail: Re-audit accepted this as non-blocking because CATALOG-013 now visibly demotes false untranslated candidates, but conflict generation beyond fixture-provided metadata is still weak source-safety coverage.

findings.impact: Future source drift could rely on fixture-authored conflicts instead of deriving conflicts from current VNDB, EGS, DLsite, or local candidate rows.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Repository-derived language conflict augmentation compares platform-language facts against existing VNDB, EGS, DLsite, and local candidate rows.
Generated conflicts preserve source provenance and do not destructively reassign external ids or language status.
Tests cover fixture-authored and repository-derived conflict paths separately.

proposedDagNode.title: Repository-derived platform-language conflict augmentation
proposedDagNode.summary: Derive official-English and platform-language conflict facts from current repository evidence rather than relying only on fixture-authored conflict payloads.
deliverables: VNDB, EGS, DLsite, local candidate comparison fixtures
Separate tests for fixture-authored and repository-derived conflict facts
acceptanceCriteria: Platform-language facts are compared against existing VNDB, EGS, DLsite, and local candidate rows to generate reviewable conflicts.
Generated conflicts preserve source provenance and never destructively merge or reassign external ids.
Opportunity or benchmark demotion explanations identify whether conflicts were fixture-authored or repository-derived.
